title: campaign-refill output data
description:
Shape of the ``data`` field on a ``campaign-refill`` envelope.

``stage_reached`` is the deterministic dispatch over the advance decision +
per-slot resolve outcomes; ``needs_decision`` is set only on
``refill_blocked`` (a slot hit a resume-vs-fresh / scaffold escalation a
human must resolve). The clean ``refilled`` / ``no_refill_needed`` terminals
end the chain — the next cron/loop tick re-enters via ``campaign-watch``
(one-step-per-tick).

stage_reached.description: Which refill outcome this tick reached.
stage_reached.title: Stage Reached

needs_decision.description: True only for refill_blocked (a slot hit a live-prior / scaffold escalation the human must resolve); False for refilled and the no_refill_needed typed no-op.
needs_decision.title: Needs Decision

reason.description: Human-readable summary of the tick's outcome.
reason.title: Reason

campaign_id.description: The campaign this refill tick operated on.
campaign_id.title: Campaign Id

decision.description: The campaign-advance decision this tick (refill / wait_in_flight / stop_* / continue) — the authoritative source refill consumed.
decision.title: Decision

refill_count.description: The pool-slots-to-fill count advance requested this tick (max(0, min(K - in_flight, remaining_max_jobs))); 0 on no_refill_needed.
refill_count.title: Refill Count

submitted.description: One row per spawned detached campaign-run child (run_id + pid + stage).
submitted.title: Submitted

blocked.description: Slots that stopped at a resume-vs-fresh / scaffold escalation before submit.
blocked.title: Blocked

next_block.description: Always null in practice: the chain ENDS at campaign-refill — the next cron/loop tick re-enters via campaign-watch (one-step-per-tick). The field is DECLARED so the MCP curated catalog derives campaign-refill as a block (a block is any verb whose Result declares next_block).
next_block.title: Next Block

active_env_overrides.description: Every framework-relevant HPC_* environment variable currently exported in THIS process's environment, verbatim (B15 disclosure; mirror CampaignRunResult). Refill spawns cluster-submitting children, so a stray transport override reshapes every submit — echoing it makes the env-vs-record drift visible. Empty when no HPC_* variable is set.
active_env_overrides.title: Active Env Overrides

BlockedSlot.description:
A refill slot that stopped at a genuine escalation before it could submit.

``resolve-submit-inputs`` returned ``prior_run_found`` (a live prior matches
this cmd_sha — only the human picks resume-vs-fresh) or
``needs_scaffold_interview`` (``.hpc/tasks.py`` absent). The refill loop
BREAKS on the first blocked slot — continuing would ask more trials against
an unresolved slot — so ``campaign-refill`` returns ``refill_blocked``.

run_id.description: The computed run_id of the blocked slot, when resolve got that far; else null.
run_id.title: Run Id

reason.description: The resolve-submit-inputs reason the human must act on.
reason.title: Reason

BlockedSlot.title: campaign-refill blocked slot

SubmittedIteration.description: One refill slot that spawned a detached ``campaign-run`` child this tick.

run_id.description: The run_id resolve-submit-inputs computed for this iteration.
run_id.title: Run Id

detached_pid.description: The detached campaign-run worker's OS process id (informational — do NOT wait on it; the outcome arrives via the journal). None if the child replayed a recorded terminal instead of spawning.
detached_pid.title: Detached Pid

stage_reached.description: The campaign-run child's stage_reached (``detached`` on a fresh spawn).
stage_reached.title: Stage Reached

SubmittedIteration.title: campaign-refill submitted iteration
